_meta.title: PLE Mathematics 2015 Uganda - V2 import package
notes: Built against the same top-level V2 import package shape as the successful KCPE Mathematics 2023 Kenya import.
No Firestore writes were performed during packaging.
Legacy image references were corrected to local public assets where a clear matching asset exists.

exam.title: PLE Mathematics 2015
exam.subject: Mathematics
exam.overallInstructions: This paper has two sections: Section A and Section B. Answer all questions and show your working where necessary.

sections.title: Section A
sections.sharedInstructions: Questions 1 to 20 carry two marks each. Answer all questions.

sections.title: Section B
sections.sharedInstructions: Questions 21 to 32 carry sixty marks in total. Answer all questions and show your working where necessary.

instructionGroups.instructionsMarkdown: Answer all questions in Section A. Give the exact number, word, or expression required.
Answer all questions in Section B. For multi-part questions, answer each labelled part separately.

items.stemMarkdown: Work out: 124 - 45
Write in figures: Eighty thousand, ten.
Simplify: 18x - 5(3x + 7)
Given that set K = {g, m, v, z}, find the number of subsets in set K.
Work out 7 - 3 on the number line below.
Find the sum of the 5th and 8th prime numbers.
Work out: 14/15 ÷ 2/5
A birthday party started at 4:30 p.m. and lasted 2¾ hours. At what time did the party end?
Show all the lines of folding symmetry in the figure below.
A trader sold a pair of shoes at sh32,800 making a profit of sh1,200. What was the cost price of the pair of shoes?
In a car park there are 192 cars. The probability that a car picked at random from the park is made in Japan is 5/8. How many cars are not made in Japan?
How many packets of 200 grammes can be got from 2.6 kilogrammes of salt?
Given that a = -2, b = 3 and c = 4, find the value of b(a² + c)
Work out: 1101₂ + 111₂
Find the size of angle y in the figure below.
The Venn diagram below shows the prime factors of 12 and 18. Find the Lowest Common Multiple (LCM) of 12 and 18.
Find the median of the numbers: 8, 10, 4, 1, 6 and 9.
Gidudu has goats and sheep in the ratio of 3:2. If he has 24 goats, how many sheep does he have?
A bucket was ¾ full of water. When 4 litres were removed, it became ½ full of water. What is the capacity of the bucket?
In a poultry farm, eggs are packed into boxes which hold 144 eggs. How many boxes of the same size are needed to pack 1,008 eggs?

In a class, 31 pupils play tennis (T) and (d + 5) play volleyball (V) only. d pupils play both games while 3 play neither of the games.

(a) Use the above information to complete the Venn diagram below.
(b) If 27 pupils play volleyball altogether, find the value of d.

(a) What number has been expanded below?
(6 × 10³) + (2 × 10¹) + (7 × 10⁰) + (3 × 10⁻²)

(b) Work out: (8.5 × 14) + (8.5 × 16)

The table below shows the rate at which different currencies were sold and bought in a commercial bank during the month of September.

Currency Exchange Rates:
• US Dollar ($): Selling 3,600 UGX | Buying 3,650 UGX
• Euro (€): Selling 4,000 UGX | Buying 4,020 UGX
• Rwandese Franc: Selling 4.0 UGX | Buying 5.0 UGX

(a) How many Euros did Musa get for Ug. Sh. 603,000?
(b) Amina came from Rwanda with 109,500 Rwandese francs and exchanged them for US dollars. How many US dollars did she get from the bank?

Betty filled container A below with drinking water. She served visitors with the water using cups of size B shown in the diagram.

Container A: Cylinder, radius = 14 cm, height = 50 cm
Cup B: Cylinder, radius = 3.5 cm, height = 10 cm

Find the total number of full cups of water she served the visitors. (Use π = 22/7)

A fruit seller sold the following number of mangoes in six days: 60, 35, 40, 28, 42 and 35.

(a) What is the modal number of mangoes sold?
(b) Work out the mean number of mangoes sold.
(c) By the end of the seventh day, the mean number of mangoes sold was 44. How many mangoes were sold on the seventh day?

In the figure below line AB is parallel to CD. Angle CTD = 44° and Angle TQR = 56°.

Find the size of:
(a) angle k
(b) angle g

The table below shows how a motor cyclist travelled from town R through towns Q and S to town P.

| Town | Arrival | Departure |
|------|---------|----------|
| R | — | 9:00 a.m. |
| Q | 9:30 a.m. | 9:42 a.m. |
| S | 10:35 a.m. | 11:10 a.m. |
| P | 1:30 p.m. | — |

(a) How long did the motor cyclist stay at town S?
(b) Find the time the motor cyclist took to travel from town R to town P.
(c) If the distance from town R to town P is 180 km, calculate the average speed of the motor cyclist for the whole journey.

Madada sold his radio to Aguti at sh. 63,000 making a loss of 10%. Aguti later sold the radio to Chebet at a profit of 15%.

(a) Calculate the amount of money Madada paid for the radio.
(b) For how much money did Aguti sell the radio?

Study the figure below and use it to answer the questions that follow.

The figure is a composite shape made of a rectangle (7m × 9m) and a triangle (base 4m, height 3m) attached to one side. The total length of one side is 10m.

(a) Calculate the area of the figure.
(b) Work out the perimeter of the figure.

Two taps F and E are connected to a water tank. Tap F can fill the tank in 2 hours while E can empty it in 3 hours. One day when the tank was ½ full of water, the taps were opened at the same time. How long did it take to fill the tank?
A geometry set costs half as much as a book. A book costs sh.600 more than a fountain pen. If the total cost of the three items is sh.6,900, find the cost of the geometry set.

A plane flew from airport K to airport T on a bearing of 120°. The distance between K and T is 600 km. It then left airport T for airport R on a bearing of 210°. The distance between T and R is 500 km.

(a) Sketch the journey made by the plane.
(b) Using a scale of 1 cm to represent 100 km draw an accurate diagram to show the journey made by the plane.
(c) Find the bearing of airport R from airport K.

interactions.promptMarkdown: Total travel time from R to P
interactions.label: (b)

markingRules.exactAnswer: T only: 31-d, V only: d+5, Both: d, Neither: 3
markingRules.notes: Manual review required because the source answer is drawing, diagram completion, or scale-sketch based.

markingRules.exactAnswer: sketch showing K to T at 120° bearing, then T to R at 210° bearing
markingRules.notes: Manual review required because the source answer is drawing, diagram completion, or scale-sketch based.

markingRules.exactAnswer: accurate diagram with K-T = 6cm at 120°, T-R = 5cm at 210°
markingRules.notes: Manual review required because the source answer is drawing, diagram completion, or scale-sketch based.

modelAnswerVersions.explanation:
124 - 45 = 79

Step-by-step:
• Borrow from tens: 14 - 5 = 9
• Borrow from hundreds: 11 - 4 = 7
• Result: 79

Set K has 4 elements.
Number of subsets = 2^n = 2^4 = 16

Start at 7 on the number line.
Move 3 steps to the left: 7 → 6 → 5 → 4
Answer: 4

Start: 4:30 p.m.
Add 2 hours: 6:30 p.m.
Add ¾ hour (45 min): 7:15 p.m.

The figure has 2 lines of symmetry:
• One vertical line through the center
• One horizontal line through the center

Selling price = sh32,800
Profit = sh1,200
Cost price = Selling price - Profit
= 32,800 - 1,200 = sh31,600

Total cars = 192
Japanese cars = 5/8 × 192 = 120
Non-Japanese cars = 192 - 120 = 72

2.6 kg = 2,600 g
Number of packets = 2,600 ÷ 200 = 13

Binary addition:
  1101
+ 0111
------
 10100

Answer: 10100₂

Ordered: 1, 4, 6, 8, 9, 10
Median = average of middle two = (6 + 8) ÷ 2 = 7

Ratio goats:sheep = 3:2
3 parts = 24 goats
1 part = 8
Sheep = 2 parts = 16

Let capacity = x litres
¾x - 4 = ½x
¾x - ½x = 4
¼x = 4
x = 16 litres

Total eggs = 1,008
Eggs per box = 144
Boxes needed = 1,008 ÷ 144 = 7

modelAnswerVersions.approvedAnswer: T only: 31-d, V only: d+5, Both: d, Neither: 3
acceptableAlternatives: T only: 31-d, V only: d+5, Both: d, Neither: 3
modelAnswerVersions.explanation:
Tennis only = 31 - d (since 31 play tennis including both)
Volleyball only = d + 5
Both = d
Neither = 3

Volleyball altogether = 27
Volleyball only + Both = 27
(d + 5) + d = 27
2d + 5 = 27
2d = 22
d = 11

Bank buys 1 Euro at sh4,020 (customer sells)
Euros = 603,000 ÷ 4,020 = 150 Euros

First convert RWF to UGX (bank buys RWF at 5.0):
109,500 × 5.0 = 547,500 UGX
Then convert to USD (bank sells USD at 3,650):
547,500 ÷ 3,650 = 150 US dollars

Volume of container A = πr²h = (22/7) × 14² × 50 = 30,800 cm³
Volume of one cup = πr²h = (22/7) × 3.5² × 10 = 385 cm³
Number of cups = 30,800 ÷ 385 = 80 cups

Mode is the most frequent value.
35 appears twice, all others appear once.
Mode = 35

Mean for 7 days = 44
Total for 7 days = 44 × 7 = 308
Total for 6 days = 240
Seventh day = 308 - 240 = 68

Angles on a straight line sum to 180°
k + 44° + 56° = 180°
k + 100° = 180°
k = 80°

Angle g and 44° are on a straight line (supplementary)
g + 44° = 180°
g = 136°

Arrival at S: 10:35 a.m.
Departure from S: 11:10 a.m.
Time at S = 11:10 - 10:35 = 35 minutes

Departure from R: 9:00 a.m.
Arrival at P: 1:30 p.m.
Time = 4 hours 30 minutes = 4.5 hours

Distance = 180 km
Time = 4.5 hours
Average speed = 180 ÷ 4.5 = 40 km/hr

Selling at 10% loss means selling at 90% of cost
90% of cost = 63,000
Cost = 63,000 × 100/90 = 70,000

Aguti bought at 63,000
Sold at 15% profit
Profit = 15% × 63,000 = 9,450
Selling price = 63,000 + 9,450 = 72,450

Area of rectangle = 7 × 9 = 63 m²
Area of triangle = ½ × 4 × 3 = 6 m²
Total area = 63 + 6 = 69 m²

Slanted side (Pythagoras): √(4² + 3²) = √25 = 5 m
Perimeter = 10 + 5 + 5 + 7 + 9 = 36 m

Tap F fills ½ tank per hour
Tap E empties ⅓ tank per hour
Net rate = ½ - ⅓ = 1/6 tank per hour
Tank is ½ full, so ½ needs to be filled
Time = ½ ÷ 1/6 = 3 hours

Let geometry set = x
Book = 2x
Fountain pen = 2x - 600
Total: x + 2x + (2x - 600) = 6,900
5x - 600 = 6,900
5x = 7,500
x = 1,500

modelAnswerVersions.approvedAnswer: sketch showing K to T at 120° bearing, then T to R at 210° bearing
acceptableAlternatives: sketch showing K to T at 120° bearing, then T to R at 210° bearing
modelAnswerVersions.explanation: Draw point K, then line at 120° (clockwise from north) for 6 cm (600 km). From T, draw line at 210° for 5 cm (500 km) to R.

modelAnswerVersions.approvedAnswer: accurate diagram with K-T = 6cm at 120°, T-R = 5cm at 210°
acceptableAlternatives: accurate diagram with K-T = 6cm at 120°, T-R = 5cm at 210°
modelAnswerVersions.explanation:
Scale: 1 cm = 100 km
K to T: 6 cm at bearing 120°
T to R: 5 cm at bearing 210°
Use protractor and ruler for accuracy.

Using scale drawing or trigonometry:
Measure the angle clockwise from north at K to the line KR.
Bearing of R from K ≈ 160°
